authenticity.notes: Authenticity check passed: article has enough specificity, business relevance, source alignment, and trend support.

notes: The article provides a practical, implementation-focused guide to building memory-enabled AI workflows in logistics.
Content is structured around a real-world use case (support triage) with measurable business outcomes.
Addresses common automation pitfalls like skipping approval steps and not tracking context.
Strong use of logistics-specific examples and clear implementation checklist.
Content is informationally sound and provides sufficient detail for a technical audience.
The article emphasizes a safe, incremental approach to automation with human oversight.
recommendedFixes: Consider adding a section on integrating memory into no-code workflow tools like Zapier or Make, as the focus is on practical implementation.
Add a brief explanation of how to set up the Notion/Airtable logging system with specific fields and structure.
Include a short architecture diagram or flow description showing how memory is stored and referenced in the AI workflow.
Add specific, tested examples of prompt structures for the AI categorization step.
Consider adding a troubleshooting example with a specific error message and how to resolve it in the workflow.
